zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Scott於1453年出生在英國肯特斯斯時，她的父親William John Scott30歲，母親Agnes Beaufitz26歲。她與Sir Edmund Bedingfield結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1514年1月29日在英國諾福爾可奧可波洛享年61歲，埋葬在英國諾福爾可奧可波洛。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Scott于1453年出生在英国肯特斯斯时，她的父亲William John Scott30岁，母亲Agnes Beaufitz26岁。她与Sir Edmund Bedingfield结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1514年1月29日在英国諾福爾可奧可波洛享年61岁，埋葬在英国諾福爾可奧可波洛。

nameMeanings.name: Scott
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish (Down): habitational and ethnic name from Middle English Scot ‘man from Scotland’. There is no evidence that the surname denoted either of the earlier senses of Scot as ‘(Gaelic-speaking) Irishman’ or ‘man from Alba’, the Gaelic-speaking region of Scotland north of the river Forth. This surname is also very common among African Americans.
English and Scottish: from the rare Middle English personal name Scot (Old English Scott, possibly also Old Norse Skotr), only certainly attested in northern England.

variants.name: Scutt
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Camille, Pierre.
English: nickname for a short person, from Middle English scut(e) ‘short’, or else for a swift runner, from Middle English scut ‘hare’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Scott was born in 1453, in Smeeth, Kent, England, her father, William John Scott, was 30 and her mother, Agnes Beaufitz, was 26. She married Sir Edmund Bedingfield. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 4 daughters. She died on 29 January 1514, in Oxborough, Norfolk, England, at the age of 61, and was buried in Oxborough, Norfolk, England.

fr.St Edmunds, Suffolk, England: St Edmund's Church, Kessingland, Suffolk, Angleterre
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Scott est née en 1453, à Smeeth, Kent, Angleterre, son père, William John Scott, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Agnes Beaufitz, avait 26 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Edmund Bedingfield. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée le 29 janvier 1514, à Oxborough, Norfolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 61 ans, et a été enterrée à Oxborough, Norfolk, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret Scott 1453 in Smeeth, Kent, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William John Scott 30 und ihre Mutter Agnes Beaufitz 26. Sie hat Sir Edmund Bedingfield geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb am 29. Januar 1514 im Alter von 61 Jahren in Oxborough, Norfolk, England und wurde in Oxborough, Norfolk, England begraben.

ko.St Edmunds, Suffolk, England: 영국 수폴크 케징글랜드 스트 에드먼드'스 처르치
ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Scott(이)가 1453년 영국 켄트 스메스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William John Scott(은)는 30세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Agnes Beaufitz(은)는 26세였다. 그녀는 Sir Edmund Bedingfield(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1514년 1월 29일 영국 노르폴크 스버루에서 61세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 노르폴크 스버루에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com Sir Edmund Bedingfield. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 4 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Scott родилась в 1453 г. в местности смит, кэнт, Англия, её отцу по имени William John Scott было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnes Beaufitz, 26. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Edmund Bedingfield. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 4 дочери. Она умерла 29 января 1514 г. в местности оксбороую, норфолк, Англия в возрасте 61 год и была похоронена в местности оксбороую, норфолк, Англия.
